title: We’re making it easier and quicker to report an error
authors: Matthew Wilson, Process Lead, Customer and Specialised Services

content.text: We’re making it easier and quicker for you to report an error in the register, title plan, copy document or search of the index map (SIM), helping you get corrections resolved faster and helping us to support you more efficiently.
Based on extensive user research with both professional customers and members of the public, we have created a clearer, more streamlined ‘Report an error’ form.
These improvements support our commitment to improving customer experience and simplifying and improving our processes.
Why we’ve created the form
Customers told us that reporting an error wasn’t always as straightforward as it could be. We’ve listened.
Our new digital form is designed to:
By gathering the right information upfront, we can reduce delays and improve average processing times.
We are also introducing new, more direct routes to access the form, making it easier to find and encouraging customers to use it as their first choice, rather than emailing or calling us.
How this will help you
Using the new form means:
Is it an error or simply an update?
If you’ve changed your name or contact address, or your mortgage has been fully paid off (‘discharged’), you should let HM Land Registry know so we can update the register
Find out how to update the register if your details have changed.
How to report an error
To give you maximum flexibility, you can reach our new ‘Report an error’ form by:
How to complete the form

When completing the online form please provide as much information as possible – for example the title number, any HM Land Registry reference and relevant documents. This helps us resolve the issue more quickly.
The new form is also available in Welsh.
What happens next

We treat requests to correct errors as a priority and aim to complete corrections within 20 working days. Some more complex cases may take longer if, for instance, we need more information to investigate the error.
We will let you know when we’ve put it right or explain why not. And if we make changes to the register or title plan as a result, we’ll send you an updated copy.
Anyone can report an error, but we may need to ask for more information if you are not the registered owner.
Find out more
Full details of the new form and how to use it are available at How to report an error.
